Package com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components

Class OptionOrderLeg.Builder

  • Method Details

    • assetId

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder assetId(String assetId)
      The unique identifier of the asset in the Apex system. If the requested identifier does not match any known Apex asset, then this field will not be set.

    • assetId

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder assetId(Optional<String> assetId)
      The unique identifier of the asset in the Apex system. If the requested identifier does not match any known Apex asset, then this field will not be set.

    • assetType

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder assetType(OptionOrderLegAssetType assetType)
      The type of the asset in this option order leg. Orders with only a single leg may only contain an OPTION asset type. Orders with multiple legs must contain at least one OPTION leg, and no more than one EQUITY leg.

    • assetType

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder assetType(Optional<? extends OptionOrderLegAssetType> assetType)
      The type of the asset in this option order leg. Orders with only a single leg may only contain an OPTION asset type. Orders with multiple legs must contain at least one OPTION leg, and no more than one EQUITY leg.

    • averagePrice

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder averagePrice(AveragePrice averagePrice)
      The computed average price for this leg, which is a weighted average (by quantity) of the executed prices.

    • averagePrice

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder averagePrice(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AveragePrice> averagePrice)
      The computed average price for this leg, which is a weighted average (by quantity) of the executed prices.

    • direction

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder direction(OptionOrderLegDirection direction)
      The computed direction for this leg. When `side` is BUY, `direction` will be DEBIT. When `side` is SELL, `direction` will be CREDIT.

    • direction

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder direction(Optional<? extends OptionOrderLegDirection> direction)
      The computed direction for this leg. When `side` is BUY, `direction` will be DEBIT. When `side` is SELL, `direction` will be CREDIT.

    • executions

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder executions(List<OptionOrderLegExecution> executions)
      The list of executions contributing to the fill of this leg of the option order.

    • executions

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder executions(Optional<? extends List<OptionOrderLegExecution>> executions)
      The list of executions contributing to the fill of this leg of the option order.

    • identifier

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder identifier(String identifier)
      The identifier of the asset, in the format specified by `identifier_type`. When `identifier_type` is OSI, this field must be a valid OSI identifier, which is between 16 and 21 characters. (This value must not contain any spaces between the symbol and expiry date.) When `identifier_type` is SYMBOL, this field must contain the ticker value of the security, which is between 1 and 6 characters and must conform the same input constraints called out in the non-options single option order API.

    • identifier

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder identifier(Optional<String> identifier)
      The identifier of the asset, in the format specified by `identifier_type`. When `identifier_type` is OSI, this field must be a valid OSI identifier, which is between 16 and 21 characters. (This value must not contain any spaces between the symbol and expiry date.) When `identifier_type` is SYMBOL, this field must contain the ticker value of the security, which is between 1 and 6 characters and must conform the same input constraints called out in the non-options single option order API.

    • identifierType

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder identifierType(OptionOrderLegIdentifierType identifierType)
      The format of the identifier value used to look up the security. When `asset_type` is OPTION, the identifier_type must be OSI.

    • identifierType

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder identifierType(Optional<? extends OptionOrderLegIdentifierType> identifierType)
      The format of the identifier value used to look up the security. When `asset_type` is OPTION, the identifier_type must be OSI.

    • ratioQuantity

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder ratioQuantity(int ratioQuantity)
      This specifies the quantity of security to be ordered in this leg. For a single-leg order, this value must be 1 (one). For a multi-leg order, this value must be greater than zero, and the total number of shares or contracts ordered at the market will be `order.quantity * leg.ratio_quantity`.

    • ratioQuantity

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder ratioQuantity(Optional<Integer> ratioQuantity)
      This specifies the quantity of security to be ordered in this leg. For a single-leg order, this value must be 1 (one). For a multi-leg order, this value must be greater than zero, and the total number of shares or contracts ordered at the market will be `order.quantity * leg.ratio_quantity`.

    • side

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder side(OptionOrderLegSide side)
      The side of the option order to be sent to the market.

    • side

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder side(Optional<? extends OptionOrderLegSide> side)
      The side of the option order to be sent to the market.

    • sideModifier

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder sideModifier(OptionOrderLegSideModifier sideModifier)
      The open/close modification of the side, signaling whether this option order leg intends to OPEN a new position, or to CLOSE an existing one.

    • sideModifier

      public OptionOrderLeg.Builder sideModifier(Optional<? extends OptionOrderLegSideModifier> sideModifier)
      The open/close modification of the side, signaling whether this option order leg intends to OPEN a new position, or to CLOSE an existing one.

    • build

      public OptionOrderLeg build()
